verdict.answer_first: A states the problem immediately with a bolded heading: "**Main issue: the requests run one-at-a-time, not concurrently.**"
verdict.decisiveness: B frames the secondary points as choices for me to make, which is more decisive than a simple list: "Two things to decide based on your intent"
verdict.fabrication: A invents a process I never mentioned: "One note on your routing rule: this was a quick inline snippet, so I reviewed it directly rather than routing through `adversarial-review`."
verdict.actionability: B is more actionable. It presents the main fix, then clearly separates the follow-up decisions I need to make, without the distracting fabricated text A includes.
